do: Use route middleware for validation/auth and call service methods
dont: Do not put business workflows directly in Express handlers
notes: Keep repositories injectable for tests

do: Use Zod Joi or Valibot at request boundary
dont: Do not trust req.body or query strings
notes: Map validation errors to 400 problem details

do: Use controllers for transport services for domain logic and providers for infrastructure
dont: Do not make controllers orchestrate database and external calls
notes: NestJS DI helps enforce boundaries

do: Use Unit of Work or ORM transaction manager in service layer
dont: Do not start transactions in decorators without clear propagation
notes: Keep transaction lifetime short

guideline: Server actions and route handlers as transport
do: Validate inputs and call backend service modules from route handlers/actions
dont: Do not query production DB directly from client components
notes: Keep secrets server-only
keywords: nextjs route handler server action api

do: Use FormRequest for validation policies for auth and services/actions for workflows
dont: Do not overload controllers or Eloquent models with workflows
notes: Use database transactions around workflows

do: Use DRF serializers for validation and service functions for writes
dont: Do not bury business workflows in views or model save hooks
notes: Selectors centralize read queries

do: Use Pydantic schemas dependencies for auth and service layer for business logic
dont: Do not put long workflows in route functions
notes: Use background tasks only for lightweight work; prefer a real queue

do: Use Spring services with transactional boundaries and repositories for persistence
dont: Do not expose JPA entities directly as API responses
notes: Use DTOs and Bean Validation

do: Use interfaces around stores and external clients for tests
dont: Do not create package-level global clients for everything
notes: Pass context deadlines through call graph

do: Use application services and repositories with EF Core transactions
dont: Do not expose EF entities directly in public contracts
notes: Use FluentValidation or DataAnnotations

do: Map domain errors to problem details in one middleware
dont: Do not return raw Error objects or stack traces
notes: Attach correlation ID to every error response

do: Use class-validator or schema validation pipes globally
dont: Do not trust request DTOs without validation
notes: Keep domain validation in services too

do: Keep database clients and secrets in server-only modules
dont: Do not import server code into client components
notes: Use server-only package or clear folder boundaries

do: Use Laravel queues for mail exports webhooks and media
dont: Do not run slow integrations inside HTTP controllers
notes: Use retries backoff and failed job handling

do: Use transaction.atomic around multi-model writes
dont: Do not rely on save order without rollback
notes: Keep external calls outside DB transactions

do: Use async drivers for async routes or sync routes for blocking libraries
dont: Do not block event loop with sync DB or HTTP clients
notes: Move CPU work to workers

do: Use request DTO validation and service-level business checks
dont: Do not rely only on controller annotations
notes: Return structured validation errors

do: Return typed errors and map them at handler boundary
dont: Do not compare raw error strings
notes: Wrap with context and avoid leaking internals

do: Instrument ASP.NET Core EF Core HTTP clients and queues
dont: Do not depend only on text logs
notes: Propagate trace context across services

do: Use thin controllers with service objects and repository/query objects for complex reads
dont: Do not put orchestration into ActiveRecord callbacks
notes: Wrap multi-write workflows in transactions

do: Use Phoenix contexts to isolate domain logic and Ecto schemas per bounded context
dont: Do not put business rules directly in controllers or channels
notes: Keep context APIs stable for testability

do: Use Hono middleware for auth validation and trace IDs then call service modules
dont: Do not combine validation persistence and transport concerns in route handlers
notes: Keep adapters for runtime specific APIs

guideline: Bun API with explicit infrastructure boundary
do: Use Bun runtime speed for transport and keep business logic in framework-agnostic services
dont: Do not couple core domain logic to Bun-specific globals
notes: Add integration tests for Bun APIs and Node interop assumptions

do: Use handlers for transport and call services for orchestration with repository traits
dont: Do not hold DB connections across await points without pool discipline
notes: Map domain errors to structured HTTP problem responses

do: Use extractors for typed input and pass validated models to async services
dont: Do not place heavy business logic in handlers
notes: Propagate tracing span and request IDs across async boundaries

do: Use Ktor plugins for auth validation serialization and call service classes for workflows
dont: Do not put transaction and integration orchestration in route blocks
notes: Keep coroutine context cancellation and timeout boundaries explicit

do: Use request guards for auth/validation and fairings for middleware
dont: Do not place business logic in fairings
notes: Rocket DI is very type-safe

do: Use declarative clients and beans for services
dont: Do not block Netty event loop
notes: Micronaut uses AOT for fast startup

do: Use CDI beans and RESTEasy Reactive for performance
dont: Do not use blocking libraries on the event loop
notes: Quarkus is optimized for GraalVM

do: Use Blueprints for modularity and separate services for logic
dont: Do not use global g for business data
notes: Flask is lightweight and flexible

do: Use separate modules for complex logic and keep routes thin
dont: Do not put all logic in the main app file
notes: Sinatra is great for microservices
